fr_barsac_chateau_coutet_premier_cru_cla.appearance: Deep golden to old-gold colour with brilliant clarity; pronounced legs indicating high viscosity and residual sugar concentration. With age, evolves toward deep amber-gold with bright, luminous reflections.
fr_barsac_chateau_coutet_premier_cru_cla.nose_summary: Immediately expressive with honey-drenched pineapple, candied orange rind, apricot, and mango. Layered floral notes of honeysuckle and orange blossom. Vanilla and saffron from 70-100% new French oak. Older vintages develop toffee, caramel, and dried-fruit complexity. A distinctive peppery, spicy note from Muscadelle is evident in some vintages.
fr_barsac_chateau_coutet_premier_cru_cla.palate_summary: Lush, generous, and creamy on entry with streaming flavours of pineapple, yellow apple, green plum, and white ginger. Candied citrus, preserved apricot, and tropical fruit dominate the mid-palate. The Barsac limestone signature delivers a knife-edge freshness (the estate name derives from 'couteau' meaning knife) that cuts through the richness, preventing cloying sweetness. Long, persistent finish with citrus zest and honeyed mineral notes.
fr_barsac_chateau_coutet_premier_cru_cla.structural_summary: Medium-plus acidity (Barsac's limestone soils deliver higher acid than most Sauternes); full body; very high residual sugar (110-150 g/L typical); 13.5% abv; pronounced viscosity; exceptionally long finish. 2014 noted as having highest combined acid + alcohol in 20+ years. Ageing potential 20-40+ years for top vintages.

de_mosel_egon_muller_scharzhofberger_bee.appearance: Burnished gold to deep old-gold colour; brilliant clarity with pronounced, viscous legs indicating extreme residual sugar concentration and low alcohol. With significant age (decades), evolves toward orange-brown hues while retaining luminosity.
de_mosel_egon_muller_scharzhofberger_bee.nose_summary: Intensely perfumed and utterly intoxicating bouquet: acacia honey, chamomile, marmalade, Japanese yuzu, candied apricot, and saffron. Characteristic Saar slate minerality underpins. With age, develops lychee, anise, green herbs, petrol/diesel notes (classic aged Riesling), and warm caramel. Delicate floral element persists throughout evolution. The bouquet is extraordinarily harmonious and never overpowering.
de_mosel_egon_muller_scharzhofberger_bee.palate_summary: Unctuous on entry with effortless concentration. Creme brulee, vanilla, quince, chamomile, orange pith, and bitter orange. The hallmark killer line of acidity offsets extreme sweetness, creating the defining Saar acid-sugar tension. While still young, can taste very sweet, but over decades develops unrivalled complexity. The wine never demands attention on the finish -- far too sophisticated for ostentation. Ridiculous length.
de_mosel_egon_muller_scharzhofberger_bee.structural_summary: Very high acidity (9-11 g/L, highest of any botrytis type); extreme residual sugar (150-220 g/L BA band); very low alcohol (7-8%); light to medium body despite sugar concentration; wet-slate minerality; finish of extraordinary persistence measured in minutes. Ageing potential: effectively limitless -- 50+ years demonstrated (1971 vintage rated 97/100 after 47 years by Vinous).

nz_marlborough_cloudy_bay_sauvignon_blan.appearance: Almost clear to pale lemon-green with water-white rim; brilliant clarity; light, fine legs indicating moderate alcohol and no residual sugar. Youthful, bright, and vibrant in the glass.
nz_marlborough_cloudy_bay_sauvignon_blan.nose_summary: Immediately expressive and enticing with passionfruit, ripe gooseberry, lime curd, kaffir/makrut lime leaf, and white nectarine. Fresh-cut grass and tomato leaf add a herbaceous dimension. Pink peppercorn, hibiscus, and orange blossom provide aromatic complexity. Citrus notes of yellow grapefruit, lemon zest, and pineapple. Crushed basil and green olive in some vintages. 2024 (40th Vintage) noted as more restrained and less tropical than previous vintages, with profound citrus, apricot, and orange blossom.
nz_marlborough_cloudy_bay_sauvignon_blan.palate_summary: Vibrant, mouthwatering intensity with concentrated, juicy tropical notes (passionfruit, pineapple, mango) melding with zesty citrus and stone fruit. Lemon curd and white peach mid-palate. Underpinned by lively grapefruit acidity carrying intense fruit to an impressively long finish. Fresh sea-salt saline edge and squeezed-lemon acidity. Red capsicum and green olive add savoury complexity. Hints of beeswax and wet stone provide textural interest.
nz_marlborough_cloudy_bay_sauvignon_blan.structural_summary: High acidity (crisp, mouthwatering); medium body; dry; 13.5% abv; moderate viscosity; impressively long finish for the style. Fresh and vibrant with no oak influence on the flagship cuvee (96%+ stainless steel, with only ~4% old French oak/concrete for texture). Drink young, within 1-3 years of vintage.
sources: https://buywinesonline.com/products/cloudy-bay-sauvignon-blanc-2025-750-ml (Wine Spectator and James Suckling notes on 2025)

ar_mendoza_catena_malbec_classic.appearance: Deep garnet to dark purple-ruby core with a narrow ruby-violet rim; mostly opaque; bright and clean. Pronounced legs indicating ripe fruit and moderate-plus alcohol.
ar_mendoza_catena_malbec_classic.nose_summary: Incredibly aromatic with prominent rose and violet florals. Ripe plum, blackberry, black cherry, and raspberry jam. Soft earthiness of browned leaves and tobacco. Red licorice and balsamic notes. Light vanilla and soft oak from French barrel ageing. A characteristic touch of initial funk on opening that blossoms quickly to reveal vibrant, saturated fruit. Some vintages show spice and herbs (cumin, pepper) alongside the dark fruit.
ar_mendoza_catena_malbec_classic.palate_summary: Rich and mouth-filling with plum, blackberry, black cherry, and raspberry. Violet florals carry through from the nose. Balsamic and soft chocolate notes from oak integration. Leathery dark fruit with earthy tobacco undertones. Juicy and fresh with vibrant fruit saturation. Medium to full body with a plush, smooth texture. The altitude freshness from high Andes vineyards (920-1450m) provides a distinctive lift and bracing vigour. Finish is dry, slightly tannic, and impressively long-lasting.
ar_mendoza_catena_malbec_classic.structural_summary: Medium-plus acidity (altitude-driven freshness); medium-plus tannins (fine-grained, soft, well-integrated); medium-plus to full body; 13-13.5% abv; dry; long finish. Cold maceration 5 days at 10C, 10-16 day ferment, 19-22 day total maceration, 11-13 months French oak (mix of new and used). Robert Parker Wine Advocate 92 points (2021 vintage). Drink now to 5-7 years.
sources: https://88bamboo.co/blogs/wine-reviews/catena-malbec-catena-zapata (detailed 2021 tasting with colour, aroma, taste, finish)

pt_vinho_verde_aveleda_casal_garcia_bran.appearance: Pale lime-juice to lemon-yellow colour with greenish tints; clear and limpid appearance; very fine, slight petillance visible as tiny bubbles clinging to glass. Light, watery legs indicating low alcohol and body.
pt_vinho_verde_aveleda_casal_garcia_bran.nose_summary: Delicate and fruity with notes of citrus (lemon, lime), green apple, and light white flowers. A subtle, youthful, fresh aroma profile that is clean and inviting without complexity. Wine Enthusiast notes it as 'all bright, clean fruit.' The nose is approachable and light, reflecting its role as an aperitif-style wine.
pt_vinho_verde_aveleda_casal_garcia_bran.palate_summary: Fresh, smooth, and well-balanced with a crisp, sprite-like quality from the slight CO2 petillance. Lemon-lime and green apple dominate, with a saline, mineral edge. Just off-dry (11 g/L RS) but refreshing rather than sweet, balanced by bracing acidity. Clean, simple, and cheerful with a short to medium-length finish. The slight fizz amplifies the refreshing character and makes it particularly agreeable chilled.
pt_vinho_verde_aveleda_casal_garcia_bran.structural_summary: High acidity (6.5 g/L tartaric, pH 2.8-3.4); light body; low alcohol (9.5%); slight petillance (added CO2); just off-dry (11 g/L RS, technically dry by EU labelling); short to medium finish. 85 points Wine Enthusiast. Drink immediately on release; not for ageing.

fr_rhone_guigal_cotes_du_rhone_rouge.appearance: Deep and dark red to purple-ruby with a narrow ruby rim; mostly opaque and bright; shiny. Good viscosity indicating ripe fruit concentration and moderate-plus alcohol.
fr_rhone_guigal_cotes_du_rhone_rouge.nose_summary: Intensely aromatic with expressive blackberry, blueberry, and black cherry fruit. Bouquet garni and garrigue herbs. Peppery spice (black pepper especially), violet florals, and a touch of campfire smoke. Notes of lilac, dark cherries, and flowers. Slight earthiness and black olive undertones. Dusty, savoury quality alongside the fruit. The elevated Syrah content (50%) drives aromatic intensity beyond typical Grenache-led Cotes du Rhone.
fr_rhone_guigal_cotes_du_rhone_rouge.palate_summary: Full, round, and 'sexy' per the producer. Smooth and supple with round, velvety tannins. Blackberry and red berry fruit with peppery spice, garrigue, and black olive. Earth and tobacco notes ground the fruit. Medium to full-bodied with remarkable harmony and textured mouthfeel. Savoury, slightly drying finish with a burst of spice at the very end and impressive length. Wine Advocate notes it as 'supple and fluid, easy to drink, with black olive and earth notes preventing simple fruit bomb.'
fr_rhone_guigal_cotes_du_rhone_rouge.structural_summary: Medium-plus acidity; medium-plus tannins (round, smooth, well-balanced); medium-plus to full body; 14-15% abv (vintage dependent); dry; long finish with elegance. 50% Syrah / 40% Grenache / 10% Mourvedre; aged 24-30 months in neutral oak foudres -- far longer than peers at this price. 90-92 points across Jeb Dunnuck, Wine Advocate, Wine Enthusiast, Decanter.
sources: https://vintus.com/wines/cotes-du-rhone-rouge/e-guigal-cotes-du-rhone-rouge-2022 (Vintus 2022 tech sheet + Wine Advocate 90, Jeb Dunnuck 91, Wine Enthusiast 91)
https://vintus.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/01/Guigal-CDR-Rouge-20-Tech.pdf (2020 tech sheet PDF with Wine Advocate 90)

pt_douro_taylor_fladgate_vintage_2017.appearance: Dark inky purple-black with a narrow brick rim and cerise highlights at the edge; nearly opaque; very dense. Pronounced viscous legs indicating high sugar, alcohol, and extract concentration.
pt_douro_taylor_fladgate_vintage_2017.nose_summary: Subdued and broody in youth -- tight and restrained. Dark inky fruit with flashes of cassis, concentrated blackcurrant, black cherry, and plum. Bold black stone fruit. Violet florals, licorice, cedar, and tobacco. Subtle menthol/eucalyptus notes. Youthful spirit evident but integrated. Described as 'quintessential Taylor -- very austere and very precise' by winemaker David Guimaraens. Opens with time to reveal greater complexity.
pt_douro_taylor_fladgate_vintage_2017.palate_summary: Powerful and structured with massive, chewy tannins that are softly textured despite their volume. Noticeably sweet on entry until the dry tannins kick in. Multiple layers of dark fruit -- blackberry preserve, Pontefract cake (licorice), creme de cassis, black plums. Chocolate, menthol, and violet notes. The palate is full-bodied, off-dry in perception despite ~100 g/L RS, and very long. Searing acidity provides lift and freshness. A hint of black pepper on the grip. Dusty, drying tannins linger with the fruit on an impressively vigorous finish.
pt_douro_taylor_fladgate_vintage_2017.structural_summary: High tannin (massive, firm, slightly austere, but fine-grained and chewy); medium-plus acidity (exceptional for the style); full body; ~100 g/L residual sugar; 20% abv; dense and concentrated; exceptionally long finish. From a universally declared vintage. Drinking window 2030+ per producer. 96-98+ points from specialist Port forums; described as 'a step up from the 2016.'

es_montilla_moriles_toro_albala_don_px_1.appearance: Dark mahogany to almost black with beautiful viscous movement in the glass; intensely concentrated, syrupy consistency. Virtually opaque at the core with a thin amber-brown rim. When poured, coats the glass thickly like molasses.
es_montilla_moriles_toro_albala_don_px_1.nose_summary: Intensely sweet with raisined fruits -- dates, plums, dried peaches, and dried figs. Roasted coffee and coffee bean notes dominate. Dark chocolate and liquorice. Orange rind and citric sensation providing lift. Balsamic and cinnamon/vanilla sweet spice. Curiously perfumed for a PX -- Wine Advocate noted 'a floral touch that at times reminded me of Moscatel.' Molasses and caramel undertones throughout.
es_montilla_moriles_toro_albala_don_px_1.palate_summary: Sweet entry that is perfectly balanced with citrus acidity and a light bitter touch, making it feel fresh despite extreme age and sweetness. Velvety and seamless with a soft, unctuous texture. Tasty, showy and easy to drink despite ~402 g/L sugar. Rich dried fruit, dark chocolate, coffee, and orange rind flavours. Wine Advocate noted it 'feels lighter and less concentrated' than expected, with an elegant drinkability. Long, persistent, lingering sweet finish with orange peel and bitter coffee notes providing complexity.
es_montilla_moriles_toro_albala_don_px_1.structural_summary: Low to medium acidity (balancing freshness from citric acid and bitter touch); full body; very sweet (~402 g/L residual sugar per Wine Advocate); very viscous; 17% abv; exceptionally long finish. Sun-dried Pedro Ximenez grapes, 7-9 days on esparto mats, 20+ years in American oak butts. 93 points Wine Advocate. 12,000 bottles + 20,000 half bottles produced.
sources: https://www.vintagewineandport.co.uk/products/Bodegas-Toro-Albal%C3%A1-1999-DON-PX-1999 (Vintage Wine and Port retailer tasting note)

au_rutherglen_morris_old_premium_rare_mu.appearance: Dark olive-brown to almost black at the core, with a distinctive green tinge on the rim (the hallmark Rare Rutherglen Muscat colour marker). Extremely thick and viscous in the glass -- coats the sides with an olive-brown stain when swirled. Virtually opaque.
au_rutherglen_morris_old_premium_rare_mu.nose_summary: Intensely scented and incredibly complex. Molasses, sandalwood, chocolate-coated coffee beans, treacle tart, and baking spices (Wine-Searcher critic composite). Raisin fruit and soft woody characters. Sous bois dampened by recent rain. Perfumed but with a delicious sharpness. Restrained despite the intensity -- the components are tightly woven and harmonious rather than individually discernible. Garrigue herbs and even a touch of peach noted by Decanter.
au_rutherglen_morris_old_premium_rare_mu.palate_summary: So thick in texture it resembles liquid raisins or Christmas pudding in a glass. Multiple layers of flavour arrive simultaneously -- rich and luscious sweet spices, dried raisins, and Christmas pudding (producer note). Dark chocolate studded with salt flakes, raisins soaked in molasses (Halliday 98pts). Incredibly sweet yet balanced by a lemony acidity and freshness throughout. Even a hint of tannin gives a dryness to the finish that counters the sweetness. Decanter rated 99/100, describing it as 'beyond fruit or chocolate flavours, mocha and espresso, garrigue herbs, powerful, sweet, almost medicinal.' The aftertaste is enormous -- one or two sips is all you need.
au_rutherglen_morris_old_premium_rare_mu.structural_summary: Medium-plus acidity (lemony freshness providing essential balance); full body; very sweet (liqueur fortified); highly viscous, oily texture; 17% abv; exceptionally long finish that lingers for minutes. Non-vintage Rare tier solera blend, average 20+ years, multi-decade material. Modified solera, seasoned oak casks. 95 points Wine Enthusiast; 98 points James Halliday; 99/100 Decanter; 100 points Halliday (previous releases); Decanter Platinum 2024; IWC Australian Fortified Trophy + Muscat Trophy 2025.
sources: https://casellafamilybrands.com/news/halliday-wine-results (James Halliday 98pts note -- dark chocolate, salt flakes, molasses)
